question: Q2
claim: Every lane emits a rankable LaneMatch[] list (skillId+numeric score), exactly the per-source ranked-list shape RRF needs.

question: Q2
claim: Lane raw-score scales are heterogeneous: lexical=hint-inflated overlap capped 1; semantic=cosine in [0.2,1]; graph_causal=signed [-1,1] — confirming the comparability problem RRF targets.

question: Q2
claim: RRF injection point is shallow: fusion already builds per-lane match index (line 341) then weighted-SUMs contributions (line 372); RRF replaces only the sum.

question: Q2
claim: CRITICAL gap confirmed: graph_causal emits SIGNED scores (conflicts_with=-0.35) but shared fuseResultsMulti only adds positive rank terms and elides zero/negative-weight lanes — naive port drops conflict suppression.

question: Q7
claim: Advisor can import the SAME fuseResultsMulti: RankedList/RrfItem shapes map 1:1 from LaneMatch; primitive already implements k, zero-weight elision, graph boost, deterministic sort, [0,1] normalization.

question: Q7
claim: Reuse caveat: primitive is monotonic-positive only (no conflict channel) and its default k=40 is corpus-tuned for ~1000 memories; advisor has far fewer skills and would pass its own smaller k via options.k.

question: Q3
claim: Lane weights are static: defaults plus a one-shot env-JSON override resolved once at module load; no path updates weights from recommendation outcomes.

question: Q3
claim: aionforge Beta posterior is bounded by evidence quality (capped at (alpha+k)/(alpha+beta+k)), cold-starts to uninformative 0.5 so nothing promotes, prior 1,1 — an anti-overfit auto-tune template.
evidence: external/aionforge-memory-development/docs/attestation-and-promotion.md (How the substrate decides; Reliability comes from elsewhere)

question: Q3
claim: Over-fitting guard for a lane auto-tune maps from aionforge's two gates: cold-start neutrality (multiplier ~1.0 until evidence), a count floor (>=k confirmed outcomes), and a clamped deviation band — keeping the static split until evidence earns deviation.

question: Q2
title: Split graph_causal into a positive RRF lane + separate post-fusion conflict re-rank signal
rationale: Preserves conflict suppression outside RRF's rank-only invariant; positive entries feed RRF (mirroring aionforge GRAPH_WEIGHT_BOOST), negatives demote after fusion.

question: Q2
title: Adopt RRF's by-construction deterministic ordering instead of toFixed(6)+localeCompare
rationale: Today's determinism is fragile under float non-associativity of a weighted sum; 1/(k+rank+1) over fixed lane order + id tiebreak is deterministic.

question: Q7
title: Import shared fuseResultsMulti (determinism-spine reuse), do not reimplement RRF
rationale: One import + lane->RankedList adapter + advisor-specific k + C1 conflict re-rank; avoids a second RRF drifting from spec-kit's.

question: Q3
title: Bounded per-lane reliability Beta posterior that nudges lane weights from confirmed outcomes
rationale: Per-lane Beta(alpha,beta) from confirmed/disconfirmed dominant-lane picks; bounded multiplier applied at effectiveScorerWeights() seam; explicit_author keeps a floor.

question: Q3
note: OPEN dependency: outcome-capture source (what signals a recommendation was 'confirmed/invoked') is unlocated; C4 needs it to become concrete.

question: Q6
note: Degrade-not-error pattern already present: semantic lane returns [] on db absent / embedder fail rather than throwing — pre-anchors Q6.
